action.reasoning: Observation: The best test_ic_mean so far is 0.015301 from a small 3-epoch MLP. The larger MLP in iter 7 failed because label_clip=0.1 destroyed signal on z-scored labels. The LGB model (iter 9) only achieved 0.013767, likely due to severe underfitting (26 trees, RMSE≈1.0) and possibly a train/test standardization mismatch. The old best MLP checkpoint was deleted in iter 9. Analysis: We need to retrain an MLP with proper settings: no label clipping (or very light), stronger regularization (dropout 0.4, weight decay 5e-4), hidden=512, and train for up to 50 epochs with early stopping on val_ic. Using SmoothL1Loss instead of MSE should make training more robust to the extreme outliers (-16 to +10) without destroying signal. We should also remove the old weak LGB checkpoint so predict.py doesn't ensemble it. Decision: Write a clean, complete train.py that trains only the MLP with these hyperparameters, delete the old LGB model, run training, test predict.py locally, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 50 --hidden_dim 512 --dropout 0.4 --weight_decay 5e-4 --patience 10 --lr 1e-3 && python predict.py --data_path local_test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints --output local_test_eval/output.json
